title: Null property values are absent — never index them, reject null MERGE keys
description: Neo4j treats a property whose value is null as absent. Mirror this: (1) never insert null into the typed property index (central no-op in PropertyIndex::add_property), so find_exact(..,Null) is always empty and null-keyed nodes can't be addressed/pollute seeks; (2) MERGE with a null property value errors "Cannot merge node using null property value for {key}", checked before match-or-create.
whenToUse: Any property-graph write/index path where Neo4j compatibility matters and null values could otherwise be stored or indexed.
whenNotToUse: Stores that intentionally treat null as a first-class, queryable value (non-Neo4j semantics).
